description_long: This is a classic cylindrical fire hydrant, painted in a weathered red color, with a domed top and prominent horizontal flanges at the base and midsection. It features three hose connection outlets: two on the sides and one facing forward, each capped with a round, bolted cover. The surface is textured with visible signs of wear, scratches, and some rust, indicating long-term outdoor exposure. The top of the hydrant has a central operating nut, used for opening the valve, and the body is reinforced with thick, raised bands. No chains are attached to the caps, and there are no visible manufacturer markings or logos. The hydrant is designed to be a fixed, robust emergency water source typically found on urban streets or sidewalks.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets, a domed top, and textured, worn surface. It is robust, cylindrical, and built for outdoor urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the domed top, central operating nut, and all three capped hose outlets, with the forward-facing outlet most prominent.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the right-side hose outlet and the cylindrical body, with the domed top and base flanges visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the hydrant and the rear-facing hose outlet, with the side outlets partially visible.
description_view_3: Angled side view from the left, focusing on the left-side hose outlet and the cylindrical body, with the domed top and base flanges visible.
